title: Trump Cut a Billion-Dollar Mining Deal. His Sons Stand to Profit.
summary: An agreement between the U.S. and Kazakhstan has given a group of American investors with ties to the president and the commerce secretary access to one of the world’s largest untapped reserves of tungsten.

title: Kazakhstan’s Leader Deepens U.S. Ties, Saying Trump Was ‘Sent by Heaven’
summary: The Central Asian nation is aggressively courting President Trump’s Washington to counterbalance its powerful neighbors, Russia and China.

title: What Life Is Like in Dahiya Amid a Hezbollah-Israel Truce in Lebanon
summary: Dahiya, a sprawling area and Hezbollah stronghold, is stirring back to life amid a tense truce with Israel. “This war has brought so much loss,” one resident said.

title: White House Secretly Swayed Board Meant to Protect Federal Workers From Unfair Firings
summary: Behind the scenes, the Trump White House went to extensive lengths to advance its theory of executive power, potentially giving the president remarkable leeway to install loyalists at nearly every echelon of government.

title: Rats, Leaks and Broken Elevators: Repair Backlog Plagues Federal Buildings
summary: After decades, deferred maintenance totals an estimated $50 billion. But getting repair funds from Congress is a laborious process.

title: Trump Picks Lance Schroyer as New ICE Director
summary: The president said he was nominating Lance Schroyer, an adviser to Markwayne Mullin, the D.H.S. secretary, to lead the high-profile agency.

title: 12 Hours With Venezuelan Doctors Searching for Earthquake Survivors
summary: A medical team set out from the capital to rescue people in the hardest-hit part of the disaster zone, La Guaira. They found silence in the ruins instead.

title: A Rush of Volunteers in Venezuela Has Slowed Rescue Efforts
summary: The outpouring of volunteer aid after Venezuela’s earthquakes clogged the only road into the disaster zone, delaying rescue crews.

title: Julia Letlow, Lifted by Trump, Wins Republican Senate Runoff in Louisiana
summary: If she reaches the Senate, Ms. Letlow would replace Senator Bill Cassidy and become the first G.O.P. woman to represent Louisiana in the chamber.

title: Colorado’s Primary for Governor Pivots to Trump and Who Will Fight Him
summary: The Democratic primary on Tuesday is likely to decide the next governor, and Senator Michael Bennet, once seen as a shoo-in, is in a dogfight with Attorney General Phil Weiser.

title: What’s the Hottest Spot in New York for Campaigning? The Club.
summary: Zohran Mamdani did it last year. So did Claire Valdez in her recent run for Congress. Some candidates are campaigning after dark to court young voters.

title: ‘After About 10 Minutes, a Car Pulled Up to the Traffic Light’
summary: Our special series of columns marking Metropolitan Diary’s 50th anniversary continues with more notable contributors sharing their New York City stories.

title: How Polymarket Traders Revolted Over One Silly Syllable
summary: How one silly syllable ignited a bitter dispute over the nature of reality on Polymarket.

title: The Real A.I. Race Isn’t America vs. China
summary: Washington is starting to follow a similar playbook to Beijing when it comes to artificial intelligence policy.

title: Dear Diary
summary: We celebrate 50 years of Metropolitan Diary.

title: A Mayor Is Taking Maternity Leave in Japan. Some Men Are Furious.
summary: Shoko Kawata is the first mayor to take maternity leave in Japan’s history. Her decision has prompted debate about gender discrimination in the work force.
